source: BG2 Pod — SpaceX IPO, AI capex, Cursor
summary: Starlink direct-to-cell and broadband can 5x revenue to ~$50B by 2028, representing only 0.3% penetration of global telecom market
summary_extended: Baker argues that Starlink can grow revenue approximately 5x from current levels to roughly $50 billion by 2028, which he frames as capturing only 0.3% of the global telecom market — a deliberately modest target given current penetration below 1% of global households. His conviction rests on Starlink's demonstrated product superiority: he cites personal experience of it being consistently the fastest and lowest-latency connection wherever he travels, and invokes the historical pattern that 'better, faster, cheaper' products tend to win at scale. The key dependency he flags is rapid Starship reusability, which is necessary to drive down cost per gigabyte and sustain the economics of mass-market broadband and direct-to-cell expansion. The investment implication is bullish on SpaceX equity, but since SpaceX remains private, there is no directly actionable US-listed equity play implied by this thesis with sufficient specificity to add.
detail: Current Starlink penetration is less than 1% of global households. Baker frames $50B as 0.3% of the global telecom market, making it achievable. Starlink is consistently the best connection (fastest, lowest latency) wherever Baker travels. Better/faster/cheaper has been a winning formula. Key dependency: rapid Starship reusability to drive down cost per gigabyte.
confidence_evidence: Baker endorses the 5x framing but qualifies it with rapid reusability dependency; uses 'better faster cheaper has been a winning formula' as conviction signal
quote_fragment: 50 billion, that's 0.3% penetration of the global telecom market
